data.text: I'll read the relevant files to understand the current state before implementing the transition matrix conformance tests.

data.output:
1	package bead
2	
3	import (
4		"fmt"
5		"testing"
6	
7		"github.com/stretchr/testify/assert"
8		"github.com/stretchr/testify/require"
9	)
10	
11	type backendConformanceCase struct {
12		name    string
13		backend string
14	}
15	
16	func backendConformanceCases() []backendConformanceCase {
17		return []backendConformanceCase{
18			{name: "jsonl", backend: BackendJSONL},
19			{name: "axon", backend: BackendAxon},
20		}
21	}
22	
23	// newBackendConformanceStore makes the backend choice explicit for the
24	// package-wide conformance matrix instead of relying on any default path.
25	func newBackendConformanceStore(t *testing.T, backend string) *Store {
26		t.Helper()
27		switch backend {
28		case BackendJSONL:
29			return newJSONLStore(t)
30		case BackendAxon:
31			return newAxonStore(t)
32		default:
33			t.Fatalf("unsupported backend %q", backend)
34			return nil
35		}
36	}
37	
38	func forEachBackendConformanceCase(t *testing.T, fn func(*testing.T, backendConformanceCase)) {
39		t.Helper()
40		for _, tc := range backendConformanceCases() {
41			tc := tc
42			t.Run(tc.name, func(t *testing.T) {
43				t.Parallel()
44				fn(t, tc)
45			})
46		}
47	}
48	
49	func runBackendConformanceSuite(t *testing.T, tc backendConformanceCase) {
50		t.Helper()
51		makeStore := func(t *testing.T) *Store { return newBackendConformanceStore(t, tc.backend) }
52	
53		t.Run("create-get", func(t *testing.T) {
54			t.Parallel()
55			s := makeStore(t)
56	
57			b := &Bead{Title: "first", Description: "round-trip"}
58			require.NoError(t, s.Create(b))
59			require.NotEmpty(t, b.ID)
60	
61			got, err := s.Get(b.ID)
62			require.NoError(t, err)
63			assert.Equal(t, "first", got.Title)
64			assert.Equal(t, "round-trip", got.Description)
65			assert.Equal(t, StatusOpen, got.Status)
66		})
67	
68		t.Run("update", func(t *testing.T) {
69			t.Parallel()
70			s := makeStore(t)
71	
72			b := &Bead{Title: "to-update"}
73			require.NoError(t, s.Create(b))
74	
75			require.NoError(t, s.Update(b.ID, func(bb *Bead) {
76				bb.Notes = "added by update"
77				bb.Priority = 3
78			}))
79	
80			got, err := s.Get(b.ID)
81			require.NoError(t, err)
82			assert.Equal(t, "added by update", got.Notes)
83			assert.Equal(t, 3, got.Priority)
84		})
85	
86		t.Run("claim", func(t *testing.T) {
87			t.Parallel()
88			s := makeStore(t)
89	
90			b := &Bead{Title: "claimable"}
91			require.NoError(t, s.Create(b))
92	
93			require.NoError(t, s.Claim(b.ID, "alice"))
94			got, err := s.Get(b.ID)
95			require.NoError(t, err)
96			assert.Equal(t, StatusInProgress, got.Status)
97			assert.Equal(t, "alice", got.Owner)
98			require.NotNil(t, got.Extra)
99			assert.NotEmpty(t, got.Extra["claimed-at"])
100	
101			err = s.Claim(b.ID, "bob")
102			require.Error(t, err)
103	
104			require.NoError(t, s.Unclaim(b.ID))
105			got, err = s.Get(b.ID)
106			require.NoError(t, err)
107			assert.Equal(t, StatusOpen, got.Status)
108			assert.Empty(t, got.Owner)
109		})
110	
111		t.Run("deps", func(t *testing.T) {
112			t.Parallel()
113			s := makeStore(t)
114	
115			root := &Bead{Title: "root"}
116			child := &Bead{Title: "child"}
117			require.NoError(t, s.Create(root))
118			require.NoError(t, s.Create(child))
119	
120			require.NoError(t, s.DepAdd(child.ID, root.ID))
121			got, err := s.Get(child.ID)
122			require.NoError(t, err)
123			require.Len(t, got.Dependencies, 1)
124			assert.Equal(t, root.ID, got.Dependencies[0].DependsOnID)
125	
126			tree, err := s.DepTree(root.ID)
127			require.NoError(t, err)
128			assert.Contains(t, tree, root.ID)
129	
130			require.NoError(t, s.DepRemove(child.ID, root.ID))
131			got, err = s.Get(child.ID)
132			require.NoError(t, err)
133			assert.Empty(t, got.Dependencies)
134		})
135	
136		t.Run("events-close", func(t *testing.T) {
137			t.Parallel()
138			s := makeStore(t)
139	
140			b := &Bead{Title: "with-events"}
141			require.NoError(t, s.Create(b))
142	
143			for i := 0; i < 3; i++ {
144				require.NoError(t, s.AppendEvent(b.ID, BeadEvent{
145					Kind:    "test",
146					Summary: fmt.Sprintf("event-%d", i),
147					Body:    fmt.Sprintf("body-%d", i),
148					Actor:   "tester",
149				}))
150			}
151	
152			events, err := s.Events(b.ID)
153			require.NoError(t, err)
154			require.Len(t, events, 3)
155			for i, e := range events {
156				assert.Equal(t, fmt.Sprintf("event-%d", i), e.Summary)
157			}
158	
159			require.NoError(t, s.Close(b.ID))
160			got, err := s.Get(b.ID)
161			require.NoError(t, err)
162			assert.Equal(t, StatusClosed, got.Status)
163			events, err = s.Events(b.ID)
164			require.NoError(t, err)
165			assert.Len(t, events, 3)
166		})
167	}
168	
169	func TestBackendConformance(t *testing.T) {
170		forEachBackendConformanceCase(t, func(t *testing.T, tc backendConformanceCase) {
171			runBackendConformanceSuite(t, tc)
172		})
173	}
174

1	package bead
2	
3	import (
4		"fmt"
5		"strings"
6		"time"
7	)
8	
9	// Bead represents a portable work item with metadata.
10	// The schema matches bd/br JSONL format for interchange compatibility.
11	// Unknown fields from external sources are preserved in Extra.
12	type Bead struct {
13		ID        string    `json:"id"`
14		Title     string    `json:"title"`
15		Status    string    `json:"status"`
16		Priority  int       `json:"priority"`
17		IssueType string    `json:"issue_type"`
18		Owner     string    `json:"owner,omitempty"`
19		CreatedAt time.Time `json:"created_at"`
20		CreatedBy string    `json:"created_by,omitempty"`
21		UpdatedAt time.Time `json:"updated_at"`
22	
23		// Optional fields (bd-compatible)
24		Labels      []string `json:"labels,omitempty"`
25		Parent      string   `json:"parent,omitempty"`
26		Description string   `json:"description,omitempty"`
27		Acceptance  string   `json:"acceptance,omitempty"`
28		Notes       string   `json:"notes,omitempty"`
29	
30		// Dependencies use bd-compatible format
31		Dependencies []Dependency `json:"dependencies,omitempty"`
32	
33		// Extra holds unknown fields for round-trip preservation.
34		// Workflow-specific fields (e.g. HELIX spec-id, execution-eligible)
35		// are stored here and written back on save.
36		Extra map[string]any `json:"-"`
37	}
38	
39	// Dependency represents a link between two beads (bd-compatible format).
40	type Dependency struct {
41		IssueID     string `json:"issue_id"`
42		DependsOnID string `json:"depends_on_id"`
43		Type        string `json:"type"` // "blocks", "related", etc.
44		CreatedAt   string `json:"created_at,omitempty"`
45		CreatedBy   string `json:"created_by,omitempty"`
46		Metadata    string `json:"metadata,omitempty"`
47	}
48	
49	// BeadEvent records append-only execution evidence.
50	type BeadEvent struct {
51		Kind      string    `json:"kind"`
52		Summary   string    `json:"summary,omitempty"`
53		Body      string    `json:"body,omitempty"`
54		Actor     string    `json:"actor,omitempty"`
55		CreatedAt time.Time `json:"created_at"`
56		Source    string    `json:"source,omitempty"`
57	}
58	
59	// Status constants
60	const (
61		StatusOpen       = "open"
62		StatusInProgress = "in_progress"
63		StatusClosed     = "closed"
64		// StatusBlocked marks an explicit external blocker that can be rechecked.
65		// Dependency waits are derived from dependency status and do not persist as
66		// blocked.
67		StatusBlocked = "blocked"
68		// StatusProposed marks work awaiting operator approval or clarification.
69		// It is outside worker dispatch until transitioned to open or cancelled.
70		StatusProposed = "proposed"
71		// StatusCancelled marks work that will not run and does not satisfy
72		// dependents.
73		StatusCancelled = "cancelled"
74	)
75	
76	// CanonicalStatuses is the single source of truth for the persisted bead
77	// status enumeration. It mirrors the bd/br canonical set documented in
78	// TD-031 §2 and the JSON Schema enum at
79	// cli/internal/bead/schema/bead-record.schema.json. The CI guard tests in
80	// sync_test.go assert that schema, TD doc, and Go source agree with this
81	// list; adding or removing a value here without updating the schema and TD
82	// will fail CI.
83	var CanonicalStatuses = []string{
84		StatusOpen,
85		StatusInProgress,
86		StatusClosed,
87		StatusBlocked,
88		StatusProposed,
89		StatusCancelled,
90	}
91	
92	// IsCanonicalStatus reports whether s is one of the persisted bead statuses
93	// in CanonicalStatuses.
94	func IsCanonicalStatus(s string) bool {
95		for _, c := range CanonicalStatuses {
96			if c == s {
97				return true
98			}
99		}
100		return false
101	}
102	
103	// IssueType constants
104	const (
105		// IssueTypeOperatorPrompt is the bead type used for operator-submitted
106		// prompts that the execute-loop runs as instructions.
107		IssueTypeOperatorPrompt = "operator-prompt"
108	)
109	
110	// Default labels and other defaults for operator-prompt beads.
111	const (
112		OperatorPromptLabelKind   = "kind:operator-prompt"
113		OperatorPromptLabelSource = "source:web-ui"
114		// OperatorPromptDefaultAcceptance is the auto-AC stub used when an
115		// operator submits a prompt without explicit acceptance criteria. The
116		// structural AC verifier is skipped for operator-prompt beads, so this
117		// stub stands as a human-readable contract only.
118		OperatorPromptDefaultAcceptance = "Agent must produce a diff or no_changes rationale; the prompt body is the contract."
119	)
120	
121	// OperatorPromptMutationGuard enforces the no-self-mutation rule from
122	// Story 15: an operator-prompt bead's execution may not create, edit, or
123	// close another operator-prompt bead. The guard returns nil when the
124	// mutation is allowed and a non-nil error when it must be rejected.
125	//
126	// actorIssueType is the issue_type of the bead currently being executed
127	// (empty when no operator-prompt context is active). targetIssueType is
128	// the issue_type of the bead about to be mutated.
129	//
130	// Allow/deny matrix:
131	//
132	//	actor=""               , target=*                → allow
133	//	actor="task"           , target=*                → allow
134	//	actor="operator-prompt", target!="operator-prompt" → allow
135	//	actor="operator-prompt", target=="operator-prompt" → deny
136	func OperatorPromptMutationGuard(actorIssueType, targetIssueType string) error {
137		if actorIssueType != IssueTypeOperatorPrompt {
138			return nil
139		}
140		if targetIssueType != IssueTypeOperatorPrompt {
141			return nil
142		}
143		return fmt.Errorf("bead: operator-prompt bead may not mutate another operator-prompt bead")
144	}
145	
146	// NewOperatorPromptBead constructs a fresh operator-prompt bead from a raw
147	// prompt string, applying the Story 15 template defaults: title is the first
148	// non-empty line of the prompt, full prompt body is preserved verbatim in the
149	// description, default labels are kind:operator-prompt + source:web-ui, the
150	// status starts in `proposed` (approval flow), priority defaults to the
151	// caller-supplied tier (clamped to MinPriority..MaxPriority), and the
152	// acceptance field carries the auto-AC stub.
153	//
154	// The returned bead is not persisted; callers feed it to Store.Create which
155	// will assign the ID and CreatedAt/UpdatedAt timestamps.
156	func NewOperatorPromptBead(prompt string, defaultTier int) *Bead {
157		body := strings.TrimSpace(prompt)
158		title := body
159		if i := strings.IndexByte(body, '\n'); i >= 0 {
160			title = strings.TrimSpace(body[:i])
161		}
162		if title == "" {
163			title = "(empty operator prompt)"
164		}
165		tier := defaultTier
166		if tier < MinPriority {
167			tier = MinPriority
168		}
169		if tier > MaxPriority {
170			tier = MaxPriority
171		}
172		return &Bead{
173			Title:       title,
174			IssueType:   IssueTypeOperatorPrompt,
175			Status:      StatusProposed,
176			Priority:    tier,
177			Labels:      []string{OperatorPromptLabelKind, OperatorPromptLabelSource},
178			Description: body,
179			Acceptance:  OperatorPromptDefaultAcceptance,
180		}
181	}
182	
183	// Default values
184	const (
185		DefaultType     = "task"
186		DefaultStatus   = StatusOpen
187		DefaultPriority = 2
188		DefaultPrefix   = "bx" // used only when repo name detection fails
189		MinPriority     = 0
190		MaxPriority     = 4
191	)
192	
193	// StatusCounts holds aggregate counts for a bead store.
194	type StatusCounts struct {
195		Open              int `json:"open"`
196		InProgress        int `json:"in_progress"`
197		Closed            int `json:"closed"`
198		Blocked           int `json:"blocked"`
199		Proposed          int `json:"proposed"`
200		Cancelled         int `json:"cancelled"`
201		Ready             int `json:"ready"`
202		NeedsHuman        int `json:"needs_human"`
203		WorkerReady       int `json:"worker_ready"`
204		DependencyWaiting int `json:"dependency_waiting"`
205		ExternalBlocked   int `json:"external_blocked"`
206		OperatorAttention int `json:"operator_attention"`
207		Total             int `json:"total"`
208	}
209	
210	// Blocker kinds surfaced through BlockedAll. These strings are part of the
211	// external DDx contract (HELIX reads them to decide how to handle a blocker).
212	const (
213		BlockerKindDependency         = "dependency"
214		BlockerKindBlockedStatus      = "blocked-status"
215		BlockerKindRetryCooldown      = "retry-cooldown"
216		BlockerKindNeedsInvestigation = "needs-investigation"
217		BlockerKindOperatorAttention  = "operator-attention"
218		BlockerKindNotEligible        = "not-execution-eligible"
219		BlockerKindSuperseded         = "superseded"
220		BlockerKindEpicOnly           = "epic-only"
221	)
222	
223	// Blocker describes why an open bead is currently not runnable. Either
224	// unclosed dependencies exist, or an execute-loop cooldown has parked the
225	// bead until NextEligibleAt.
226	type Blocker struct {
227		Kind           string   `json:"kind"`
228		NextEligibleAt string   `json:"next_eligible_at,omitempty"`
229		UnclosedDepIDs []string `json:"unclosed_dep_ids,omitempty"`
230		LastStatus     string   `json:"last_status,omitempty"`
231		LastDetail     string   `json:"last_detail,omitempty"`
232		Reason         string   `json:"reason,omitempty"`
233	}
234	
235	// BlockedBead pairs a bead with its blocker classification.
236	type BlockedBead struct {
237		Bead
238		Blocker Blocker `json:"blocker"`
239	}
240	
241	// DepIDs returns a flat list of dependency IDs for this bead.
242	func (b *Bead) DepIDs() []string {
243		var ids []string
244		for _, d := range b.Dependencies {
245			ids = append(ids, d.DependsOnID)
246		}
247		return ids
248	}
249	
250	// HasDep returns true if the bead depends on the given ID.
251	func (b *Bead) HasDep(id string) bool {
252		for _, d := range b.Dependencies {
253			if d.DependsOnID == id {
254				return true
255			}
256		}
257		return false
258	}
259	
260	// AddDep adds a dependency if it doesn't already exist.
261	func (b *Bead) AddDep(depID, depType string) {
262		for _, d := range b.Dependencies {
263			if d.DependsOnID == depID {
264				return // already exists
265			}
266		}
267		b.Dependencies = append(b.Dependencies, Dependency{
268			IssueID:     b.ID,
269			DependsOnID: depID,
270			Type:        depType,
271			CreatedAt:   time.Now().UTC().Format(time.RFC3339),
272		})
273	}
274	
275	// RemoveDep removes a dependency by target ID.
276	func (b *Bead) RemoveDep(depID string) {
277		var filtered []Dependency
278		for _, d := range b.Dependencies {
279			if d.DependsOnID != depID {
280				filtered = append(filtered, d)
281			}
282		}
283		b.Dependencies = filtered
284	}
285

data.text: Now let me look at the existing test helpers and the lifecycle_transition_store_test.go:

data.output:
1	package bead
2	
3	import (
4		"testing"
5	
6		"github.com/stretchr/testify/assert"
7		"github.com/stretchr/testify/require"
8	)
9	
10	func TestLifecycleTransitionWriteAPI_AllowsDocumentedTransitions(t *testing.T) {
11		tests := []struct {
12			name string
13			from string
14			to   string
15			opts LifecycleTransitionOptions
16		}{
17			{name: "proposed to open", from: StatusProposed, to: StatusOpen},
18			{name: "proposed to cancelled", from: StatusProposed, to: StatusCancelled},
19			{name: "open to in progress", from: StatusOpen, to: StatusInProgress},
20			{name: "in progress to open", from: StatusInProgress, to: StatusOpen},
21			{name: "in progress to closed", from: StatusInProgress, to: StatusClosed},
22			{name: "in progress to proposed", from: StatusInProgress, to: StatusProposed, opts: LifecycleTransitionOptions{OperatorRequired: true}},
23			{name: "open to blocked", from: StatusOpen, to: StatusBlocked, opts: LifecycleTransitionOptions{ExternalBlockerReason: "waiting for upstream"}},
24			{name: "blocked to open", from: StatusBlocked, to: StatusOpen},
25			{name: "closed to open manual reopen", from: StatusClosed, to: StatusOpen, opts: LifecycleTransitionOptions{ManualReopen: true}},
26		}
27	
28		for _, tt := range tests {
29			t.Run(tt.name, func(t *testing.T) {
30				s := newTestStore(t)
31				b := &Bead{Title: tt.name, Status: tt.from}
32				require.NoError(t, s.Create(b))
33	
34				require.NoError(t, s.SetLifecycleStatus(b.ID, tt.to, tt.opts))
35				got, err := s.Get(b.ID)
36				require.NoError(t, err)
37				assert.Equal(t, tt.to, got.Status)
38				if tt.to == StatusBlocked {
39					assert.Equal(t, tt.opts.ExternalBlockerReason, got.Extra[ExtraLifecycleExternalBlockerReason])
40				}
41			})
42		}
43	}
44	
45	func TestLifecycleTransitionWriteAPI_RejectsInvalidTransitions(t *testing.T) {
46		s := newTestStore(t)
47		b := &Bead{Title: "guarded"}
48		require.NoError(t, s.Create(b))
49	
50		require.Error(t, s.SetLifecycleStatus(b.ID, "needs_human", LifecycleTransitionOptions{}))
51		require.Error(t, s.SetLifecycleStatus(b.ID, "needs_investigation", LifecycleTransitionOptions{}))
52		require.Error(t, s.SetLifecycleStatus(b.ID, StatusBlocked, LifecycleTransitionOptions{}))
53	
54		got, err := s.Get(b.ID)
55		require.NoError(t, err)
56		assert.Equal(t, StatusOpen, got.Status)
57		assert.False(t, LifecycleStatusSatisfiesDependency(StatusCancelled))
58	}
59	
60	func TestLifecycleTransitionWriteAPI_RejectsDirectUpdateBypass(t *testing.T) {
61		s := newTestStore(t)
62		b := &Bead{Title: "direct status write"}
63		require.NoError(t, s.Create(b))
64	
65		err := s.Update(b.ID, func(b *Bead) {
66			b.Status = StatusInProgress
67		})
68		require.Error(t, err)
69		assert.Contains(t, err.Error(), "requires Store.TransitionLifecycle")
70	
71		got, getErr := s.Get(b.ID)
72		require.NoError(t, getErr)
73		assert.Equal(t, StatusOpen, got.Status)
74	}
75

1	package bead
2	
3	import (
4		"bufio"
5		"encoding/json"
6		"fmt"
7		"os"
8		"path/filepath"
9		"strings"
10		"sync"
11		"sync/atomic"
12		"testing"
13		"time"
14	
15		"github.com/stretchr/testify/assert"
16		"github.com/stretchr/testify/require"
17	)
18	
19	// TestMain scrubs all GIT_* environment variables before running tests so
20	// test subprocesses don't inherit lefthook's GIT_DIR/GIT_WORK_TREE and leak
21	// into the parent repo's config.
22	func TestMain(m *testing.M) {
23		for _, kv := range os.Environ() {
24			if strings.HasPrefix(kv, "GIT_") {
25				if idx := strings.IndexByte(kv, '='); idx >= 0 {
26					_ = os.Unsetenv(kv[:idx])
27				}
28			}
29		}
30		os.Exit(m.Run())
31	}
32	
33	func newTestStore(t *testing.T) *Store {
34		t.Helper()
35		dir := t.TempDir()
36		s := NewStore(filepath.Join(dir, ".ddx"))
37		require.NoError(t, s.Init())
38		return s
39	}
40	
41	func newConfiguredStore(t *testing.T, backend string) *Store {
42		t.Helper()
43		dir := t.TempDir()
44		if backend != "" {
45			writeStoreConfig(t, dir, backend)
46		}
47		s := NewStore(filepath.Join(dir, ".ddx"))
48		require.NoError(t, s.Init())
49		return s
50	}
51	
52	func newJSONLStore(t *testing.T) *Store {
53		t.Helper()
54		return newConfiguredStore(t, BackendJSONL)
55	}
56	
57	func writeStoreConfig(t *testing.T, dir string, backend string) {
58		t.Helper()
59		ddxDir := filepath.Join(dir, ".ddx")
60		require.NoError(t, os.MkdirAll(ddxDir, 0o755))
61		content := fmt.Sprintf(`version: "1.0"
62	library:
63	  path: "./library"
64	  repository:
65	    url: "https://github.com/test/repo"
66	    branch: "main"
67	bead:
68	  backend: %s
69	`, backend)
70		require.NoError(t, os.WriteFile(filepath.Join(ddxDir, "config.yaml"), []byte(content), 0o644))
71	}
72	
73	func TestInit(t *testing.T) {
74		dir := t.TempDir()
75		s := NewStore(filepath.Join(dir, ".ddx"))
76		require.NoError(t, s.Init())
77	
78		_, err := os.Stat(s.File)
79		assert.NoError(t, err, "beads.jsonl should exist after init")
80	}
81	
82	func TestInitUsesCollectionFile(t *testing.T) {
83		dir := t.TempDir()
84		s := NewStoreWithCollection(filepath.Join(dir, ".ddx"), "exec-runs")
85		require.Equal(t, "exec-runs", s.Collection)
86		require.Equal(t, filepath.Join(dir, ".ddx", "exec-runs.jsonl"), s.File)
87		require.Equal(t, filepath.Join(dir, ".ddx", "exec-runs.lock"), s.LockDir)
88		require.NoError(t, s.Init())
89	
90		_, err := os.Stat(s.File)
91		assert.NoError(t, err, "collection file should exist after init")
92	}
93	
94	func TestNewStore_DefaultsToJSONL(t *testing.T) {
95		s := NewStore(filepath.Join(t.TempDir(), ".ddx"))
96		assert.Nil(t, s.backend)
97	}

100		tempDir := t.TempDir()

19	// newAxonStore returns a *Store wired to a fresh AxonBackend rooted at a
20	// temp dir. Mirrors newTestStore so the existing high-level Backend method
21	// surface (Create/Get/Update/Claim/Close/...) gets exercised against the
22	// axon-shaped storage layout instead of the JSONL default.
23	func newAxonStore(t *testing.T) *Store {
24		s, _ := newAxonStoreWithTransport(t)
25		return s
26	}
27	
28	func newAxonStoreWithTransport(t *testing.T) (*Store, *fakeAxonGraphQLTransport) {
29		t.Helper()
30		dir := filepath.Join(t.TempDir(), ".ddx")
31		transport := newFakeAxonGraphQLTransport()
32		s := NewStore(dir)
33		s.backend = NewAxonBackend(dir, s.LockWait,
34			WithAxonGraphQLTransport(transport),
35			WithAxonGraphQLClient(transport),
36		)
37		require.NoError(t, s.Init())
38		return s, transport
39	}
40	
41	type fakeAxonGraphQLTransport struct {
42		mu     sync.Mutex
43		beads  []axonEntityEnvelope
44		events []axonEventEnvelope
45	}
46	
47	func newFakeAxonGraphQLTransport() *fakeAxonGraphQLTransport {
48		return &fakeAxonGraphQLTransport{}
49	}
50	
51	func (t *fakeAxonGraphQLTransport) Query(_ context.Context, query string, variables map[string]any, response any) error {
52		t.mu.Lock()
53		defer t.mu.Unlock()
54		switch query {
55		case axonReadCorpusQuery:
56			if response == nil {
57				return nil
58			}
59			resp, ok := response.(*axonCorpusResponse)
60			if !ok {
61				return fmt.Errorf("unexpected response type %T", response)
62			}
63			resp.Beads = append(resp.Beads[:0], t.beads...)
64			resp.Events = append(resp.Events[:0], t.events...)
65			return nil
66		case axonWriteCorpusMutation:
67			var beads []axonEntityEnvelope
68			var events []axonEventEnvelope
69			if raw, ok := variables["beads"]; ok {
70				if err := decodeJSONValue(raw, &beads); err != nil {
71					return err
72				}
73			}
74			if raw, ok := variables["events"]; ok {
75				if err := decodeJSONValue(raw, &events); err != nil {
76					return err
77				}
78			}
79			t.beads = append(t.beads[:0], beads...)
80			t.events = append(t.events[:0], events...)

data.text: The `blocked-from` tests fail because `got.Extra` is nil after the transition (the empty Extra map isn't preserved through serialization). Let me look at the marshaling:

The issue is clear: after the last key is deleted from `Extra`, the round-trip through JSONL produces a `nil` Extra map (no unknown fields to populate it). The `require.NotNil(t, got.Extra)` assertion is wrong — a nil map means the key is certainly absent (which is what we want to check). Fix:

input.old_string:
got, err := s.Get(b.ID)
				require.NoError(t, err)
				assert.Equal(t, lc.toStatus, got.Status, "status must be persisted")
				require.NotNil(t, got.Extra)
				assert.Empty(t, got.Extra[ExtraLifecycleExternalBlockerReason],
					"ExtraLifecycleExternalBlockerReason must be cleared after leaving blocked")

data.error:

				require.NoError(t, err)
				assert.Equal(t, lc.toStatus, got.Status, "status must be persisted")
				require.NotNil(t, got.Extra)
				assert.Empty(t, got.Extra[ExtraLifecycleExternalBlockerReason],
					"ExtraLifecycleExternalBlockerReason must be cleared after leaving blocked")</tool_use_error>

					assert.Empty(t, got.Extra[ExtraLifecycleExternalBlockerReason],
						"ExtraLifecycleExternalBlockerReason must be cleared after leaving blocked")

git commit -m "$(cat <<'EOF'
tests: add transition-matrix coverage to backend_conformance_test [ddx-94a368b6]

Extends backend_conformance_test.go with TestBackendConformance_TransitionMatrix
that walks every legal transition declared in lifecycle.go:244-256 against both
the JSONL and Axon backends, asserting Status persistence and Extra side effects
(ExtraLifecycleExternalBlockerReason set/cleared, claim fields cleared on Reopen).
Also asserts forbidden transitions (closed->*, cancelled->*, in_progress->cancelled)
return ValidateLifecycleTransition errors without mutating persisted status.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
)"

data.text:
Commit `2c371871a` is in. All ACs verified:

1. **AC1** — `TestBackendConformance_TransitionMatrix` in `backend_conformance_test.go` walks every legal transition from `lifecycle.go:244-256` (`legal` + `blocked-from` sub-tests) and asserts Status + Extra side effects against both backends.

2. **AC2** — `forbidden` sub-test asserts `closed->*`, `cancelled->*`, and `in_progress->cancelled` all return errors from `SetLifecycleStatus` (which calls `ValidateLifecycleTransition`) and leave status unchanged.

3. **AC3** — `forEachBackendConformanceCase` runs everything against both `jsonl` and `axon` backends.

4. **AC4** — `go test ./internal/bead/... -run TestBackendConformance` green (0.147s).

5. **AC5** — `go test ./internal/bead/... ./internal/agent/... ./cmd/...` all green.

6. **AC6** — `lefthook run pre-commit` passed (all 13 checks ✔).
